evidence.scientific_clarity: 10x10 confusion matrix with dual colormaps (YlGn diagonal, Blues off-diagonal), all cells annotated, axes labeled True/Predicted — fully satisfies the brief.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype set; PDF embeds DejaVuSans and DejaVuSans-Bold as Type3 (bitmapped glyphs), not Type42/TrueType; default DejaVu Sans font used throughout.
evidence.palette: Blues (sequential, colorblind-safe) for off-diagonal and YlGn for diagonal are both CB-safe; no red/green directional misuse; dual-cmap design is intentional and documented.
evidence.layout_density: 7×6 in figure with two right-side colorbars and tight_layout(); cell annotations at 7.5–9 pt are small but workable; no obvious overlap or gaping whitespace.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption accurately describes both colormaps and matches the script; numerical claim (69–92% accuracy) is present; a reviewer may question the dual-colormap necessity vs. a single unified scale.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF confirmed to embed fonts as /Subtype /Type3 (bitmapped), not Type42; text is not selectable/editable; script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (26 KB), figure.png (64 KB), caption.tex (393 B), gen_figure.py (3 KB); bonus figure.svg also produced.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states structure (rows=true, columns=predicted), names both colormaps, and provides a specific numerical finding (per-class accuracy 69% C1 to 92% C8).

overall_strengths: All required output files are present and non-trivial; bonus SVG also generated.
Caption is specific and informative, with a concrete numerical accuracy range that matches the figure design.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype) never set — PDF text is Type3 bitmapped glyphs, making it non-selectable and unsuitable for publication.
Default DejaVu Sans font used with no explicit rcParams configuration, signalling a non-publication-ready typography setup.
